title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

title: ‘It’s Undignified’: Hundreds of Workers Training Meta’s AI Could Be Laid Off
description: More than 700 people working for a Meta contractor in Ireland are at risk of losing their jobs, documents show.

title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

title: Humanoid robots start sorting luggage in Tokyo airport test amid labor shortage
description: Humanoid robots could load cargo and clean aircraft cabins at Haneda Airport.

title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

title: GitHub will start charging Copilot users based on their actual AI usage
description: GitHub says it can no longer absorb "escalating inference cost" from it heaviest AI users.

title: Electrical current might be the key to a better cup of coffee
description: University of Oregon scientists repurposed battery-testing tool to better measure coffee's flavor profile

title: The great American data center divide
description: Many rural communities are viscerally opposed to AI infrastructure.

title: A billion miles in less than a decade: GM's Super Cruise reaches a milestone
description: The hands-free, eyes-on driver assist only works on geofenced highways.

title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

title: The Race Is on to Keep AI Agents From Running Wild With Your Credit Cards
description: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.

title: The Bloomberg Terminal Is Getting an AI Makeover, Like It or Not
description: WIRED spoke with Bloomberg’s chief technology officer about the big, chatbot-style changes coming to the iconic platform for traders.

title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk
description: Musk’s lawsuit challenges OpenAI’s evolution under Sam Altman. But during jury selection, several potential jurors voiced negative views of Musk himself.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
